🔥 Breaking Security News

📋 Security Roundup Feb 10–16 2026: AI Infrastructure Under Fire, Cloud Misconfigs & $4.3M in Bug Bounties

Published: February 17, 2026 • ~2,600 words • 12 min read • 📋 WEEKLY ROUNDUP

The week AI infrastructure became the primary attack target. CVE-2026-22778 (vLLM CVSS 9.8), six n8n CVEs, Azure Functions info disclosure, a real-world 10-minute AWS cloud breach, HackerOne's $4.3M Live Hacking week, and the five patterns defining 2026 security.

What you'll learn:

  • vLLM & n8n critical CVEs — what to test and how to report responsibly
  • Azure Functions credential exposure patterns (CVE-2026-21532)
  • Real AWS breach: 10 minutes from leaked IAM creds to 19 compromised principals
  • Django ASGI DoS, Chrome SOP bypass, SAP API exposure
  • HackerOne $4.3M payout + Bugcrowd AI triage + Chime double bounties
  • 5 essential tools + 4 must-read books with affiliate links
Read Weekly Roundup →

🚨 VS Code Extensions Vulnerabilities: 125 Million Developers At Risk — Three CVEs Still Unpatched

Published: February 19, 2026 • ~3,500 words • 10 min read • 🚨 BREAKING — THREE UNPATCHED CVEs

Four critical vulnerabilities in Live Server, Markdown Preview Enhanced, Code Runner, and Microsoft Live Preview affect 125M+ installs. CVE-2025-65717 (CVSS 9.1) lets any malicious website silently exfiltrate your local files while Live Server is running. Three CVEs remain unpatched with no response from maintainers.

What you'll learn:

  • CVE-2025-65717: How Live Server exposes your project files to any website you visit
  • CVE-2025-65716: Arbitrary JS execution via crafted markdown files
  • CVE-2025-65715: RCE via poisoned .vscode/settings.json
  • Real-world attack scenarios for each vulnerability
  • Immediate mitigations and long-term hardening steps
  • What this means for developer-environment bug bounty hunting
🔥 Read Critical Analysis →

🚨 Critical n8n Vulnerability: Six CVEs Expose Workflow Automation to RCE

Published: February 11, 2026 • 5,900 words • 9 min read • CRITICAL - CVSS 9.4

Six critical vulnerabilities in one day. CVE-2026-25049 scores 9.4 CVSS. Type confusion sandbox escape enables RCE. n8n workflow automation hit with devastating security disclosure affecting thousands of deployments.

What you'll learn:

  • CVE-2026-25049: Type confusion sandbox escape exploitation
  • JavaScript sandbox bypass techniques (prototype pollution)
  • Five additional CVEs: XSS, auth bypass, path traversal, SQLi, SSTI
  • Credential theft and cloud infrastructure compromise
  • Detection strategies and remediation steps
  • Upgrade to 1.123.17+ or 2.5.2+ immediately
🔥 Read Critical Analysis →

🚨 Critical n8n Vulnerability: CVSS 10.0 RCE Threatens Cloud Workflow Platforms

Published: February 8, 2026 • 5,400 words • 8 min read • CRITICAL - CVSS 10.0

Perfect CVSS 10.0 score. Unauthenticated RCE. Complete server takeover. CVE-2026-21858 in n8n workflow automation platform is one of the most critical cloud vulnerabilities of 2026.

What you'll learn:

  • Content-type confusion vulnerability exploitation in webhooks
  • Attack chain: Arbitrary file read → Session forgery → RCE
  • How to test for content-type confusion in APIs
  • Cloud infrastructure lateral movement scenarios (AWS/Azure/GCP)
  • Immediate mitigation steps for n8n users
  • Why this pattern matters for bug bounty hunters
🔥 Read Critical Analysis →

CVE-2026-22778: Critical vLLM RCE Vulnerability Threatens AI Infrastructure

February 10, 2026 • 🚨 BREAKING - CVSS 9.8 CRITICAL

Critical remote code execution vulnerability in vLLM LLM serving framework. Default installations vulnerable to unauthenticated RCE affecting GPU clusters.

🚨 ChainLeak: AI Framework Vulnerabilities Enable Enterprise Cloud Takeovers

Published: February 8, 2026 • 4,800 words • 7 min read • BREAKING NEWS

Critical CVE-2026-22218 and CVE-2026-22219 vulnerabilities discovered in Chainlit AI framework enable cloud takeovers. Arbitrary file read and SSRF vulnerabilities allow attackers to steal AWS credentials and access enterprise infrastructure.

What you'll learn:

  • How ChainLeak enables AWS cloud takeover in under 10 minutes
  • Arbitrary file read exploitation (CVE-2026-22218)
  • SSRF to AWS IMDS credential theft (CVE-2026-22219)
  • Testing AI frameworks for security vulnerabilities
  • Snort/WAF detection rules for ChainLeak
  • Cloud hardening: IMDSv2, least privilege IAM, network segmentation
Read Full Analysis →

🚨 LinkedIn API Access Control Bypass - BOLA Testing Guide

Published: February 8, 2026 • 3,200 words • 6 min read

Two major access control bugs just disclosed on LinkedIn's HackerOne program. Learn how to find BOLA/IDOR vulnerabilities in GraphQL APIs and premium feature bypasses.

What you'll learn:

  • How LinkedIn's comment permission bypass worked
  • Premium feature access via GraphQL manipulation
  • Step-by-step BOLA testing methodology
  • GraphQL-specific testing techniques
  • Essential tools (Burp Suite, Postman, Autorize)
  • Bounty ranges for BOLA bugs ($500-10,000)
Read Analysis →

🔧 Best Security Testing Tools for Bug Bounty Hunters 2026

Published: February 8, 2026 • 3,000 words • 15 min read

Comprehensive review of the 12 essential security testing tools every bug bounty hunter needs. Covers web application scanners, API testing tools, reconnaissance platforms, and automation frameworks.

Tools reviewed:

  • Burp Suite Professional vs OWASP ZAP
  • Nuclei (7,000+ vulnerability templates)
  • ProjectDiscovery Suite (Subfinder, Httpx, Katana)
  • Postman & GraphQL tools
  • Shodan for reconnaissance
  • Cloud security tools (ScoutSuite, Prowler)
